description_long: This sand patch consists of a roughly rectangular section of brown, granular terrain with a prominent, irregular crack or depression running diagonally across its surface. The surface is uneven, with one side slightly elevated and the other sloping downward, creating a visible step or ledge. The coloration is predominantly earthy brown, interspersed with lighter speckles and subtle variations in tone, suggesting a mix of fine sand and small stones. The edges of the patch are jagged and irregular, giving the impression of a naturally occurring break or erosion in an otherwise flat ground. The depression is shaded darker, indicating depth and a possible accumulation of finer, looser material. This type of sand patch is commonly found in urban construction sites, roadside repairs, or areas where the ground has been disturbed or partially excavated.
description: A brown, irregular sand patch with a diagonal depression and jagged edges, featuring subtle speckles and a mix of fine sand and small stones. The surface is uneven, with a visible step and shaded crevice.
description_view_0: The sand patch is seen from above and slightly to the side, highlighting the deep diagonal depression and the uneven, stepped surface.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the length of the depression and the sloping transition from the higher to lower section of the patch.
description_view_2: The patch appears flatter from this angle, with the depression less pronounced and the surface texture more visible.
description_view_3: The depression is visible but appears shallower, and the jagged edge on the right side is more prominent.
attribute_location: construction site or roadside repair area
attribute_boundary_definition: The boundaries are irregular and jagged, with some areas appearing sharply defined while others blend more gradually into the surrounding surface.
attribute_presence_of_debris: minimal, with only small stones and speckles visible
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: The sand patch appears partially blended with its surroundings, with some edges merging into adjacent terrain and others standing out more distinctly.
attribute_evidence_of_recent_disturbance: minor, as indicated by the uneven surface and depression suggesting recent excavation or ground movement
